creator: bms@incunabulum.net
text:
Sean C. Farley wrote:
>
> I have compat6x-i386-6.4.604000.200810 installed on my i386 system.

Same here on both systems.

>
>> On i386, it craps out with a bunch of "Cannot allocate red zone for 
>> initial thread" at line 384 in .../ libthr/thr/thr_init.c  errno 12 
>> messages.
>
> I recall errors like that with some other application that was getting 
> mixed thread libraries.  Do you have an /etc/libmap.conf file?

No libmap.conf file on the affected amd64 system.
The libmap.conf file on the affectde i386 system only contains entries 
for JRE and browser plugins, etc.

>
>> I am newly portupgrade'd as of 00:00 UTC *today* with the new X 
>> libraries.  I am using 7.1-PRERELEASE from 23 Dec on both boxes.
>
> My ports are about a week old before the xorg update with a 7-STABLE 
> from the same time frame.

Things are pretty bleeding edge here, and I have had a *lot* of problems 
with USB mice and the new Xorg server, I have even had hald completely 
hang my 7.1-STABLE system.

>
>> I temporarily installed to /tmp/xxx PREFIX for testing.
>>
>> Any ideas?
>
> I just tried 184363 on my i386 system without any problems.  What does 
> this give you:  P4VLDD=1 /tmp/localtest/bin/p4v
empiric:~/y/ports/devel/p4v % env P4VLDD=1 /tmp/xxx/bin/p4v
/tmp/xxx/bin/p4v.bin:
        libQtAssistantClient.so.4 => 
/tmp/xxx/lib/p4v/qt4/lib/libQtAssistantClient.so.4 (0x2897d000)
        libQtXml.so.4 => /tmp/xxx/lib/p4v/qt4/lib/libQtXml.so.4 (0x28983000)
        libQtNetwork.so.4 => /tmp/xxx/lib/p4v/qt4/lib/libQtNetwork.so.4 
(0x289bd000)
        libQtGui.so.4 => /tmp/xxx/lib/p4v/qt4/lib/libQtGui.so.4 (0x28a59000)
        libQtCore.so.4 => /tmp/xxx/lib/p4v/qt4/lib/libQtCore.so.4 
(0x2923e000)
        libpthread.so.2 => /lib/libpthread.so.2 (0x2941a000)
        libc.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/compat/libc.so.6 (0x2943f000)
        libm.so.4 => /usr/local/lib/compat/libm.so.4 (0x2952f000)
        libSM.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libSM.so.6 (0x29545000)
        libICE.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libICE.so.6 (0x2954d000)
        libXrender.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libXrender.so.1 (0x29563000)
        libXrandr.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libXrandr.so.2 (0x2956c000)
        libXcursor.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libXcursor.so.1 (0x29572000)
        libfreetype.so.9 => /usr/local/lib/libfreetype.so.9 (0x2957b000)
        libfontconfig.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libfontconfig.so.1 (0x295ec000)
        libXext.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXext.so.6 (0x29615000)
        libX11.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libX11.so.6 (0x29623000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x2970a000)
        libstdc++.so.5 => /usr/lib/libstdc++.so.5 (0x297ff000)
        libuuid.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libuuid.so.1 (0x298ca000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x298ce000)
        libXfixes.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libXfixes.so.3 (0x299d0000)
        libz.so.4 => /lib/libz.so.4 (0x299d5000)
        libexpat.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libexpat.so.6 (0x299e7000)
        libXau.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXau.so.6 (0x29a07000)
        libxcb.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libxcb.so.2 (0x29a0a000)
        librpcsvc.so.4 => /usr/lib/librpcsvc.so.4 (0x29a21000)
        libXdmcp.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXdmcp.so.6 (0x29a29000)



> Do you see multiple thread libraries?

I see nothing I recognise as such in the above output, however I see 
multiple libc's.

I also see libxcb.so.2....

>   Is P4VRES set?  I do not have it set on my system.
>

Yes. I tried unsetting it first and this didn't help.

Before unsetting P4VRES I did however get a core dump and a backtrace:
(gdb) bt
#0  0x2943755b in pthread_testcancel () from /lib/libpthread.so.2
#1  0x2942feec in pthread_mutexattr_init () from /lib/libpthread.so.2
#2  0x2952c490 in ?? ()

If I then unset P4VRES I get this:
#0  0x2943755b in pthread_testcancel () from /lib/libpthread.so.2
#1  0x29a137ba in xcb_wait_for_reply () from /usr/local/lib/libxcb.so.2
#2  0x2966337a in _XReply () from /usr/local/lib/libX11.so.6
#3  0x29642156 in XGetWindowProperty () from /usr/local/lib/libX11.so.6

I just did the libxcb bump over the weekend.

I strongly suspect this is a libxcb related issue?  However p4v.bin 
appears to be picking up both the 6 and 7 libc's.

Afaik X libraries are not part of compat6x, but if port is picking up X 
libraries from what's installed on the system, any dependency on system 
libc is going to be brought in, right?

BTW: I may not have a lot of free time to look at this. The bombs from 
the Xorg upgrade ate time which was earmarked for other stuff. So if you 
are happy to take maintainership, just give me a shout.
